description: Evaluates the transformation roadmap skill across different project scales, budget ranges, and governance scenarios. Each eval targets a specific capability of phased execution planning.

evals.name: standard-18month-transformation
evals.prompt: Create a roadmap for migrating our monolithic ERP to microservices. Duration: 18 months, budget: 2m-5m.
evals.expected_output: Full 7-section roadmap with 5 phases (Foundation through Scale), investment horizon with 3-year TCO, team ramp-up, estimation pivot points with PoC validation criteria, risk management, and governance framework.
expectations: All 5 phases defined with explicit GO/NO-GO gate criteria
Investment horizon includes 3-year TCO with phased funding model and kill points
At least 5 estimation pivot points with PoC validation criteria
Team ramp-up shows month-by-month headcount with role introduction sequence
Risk timeline shows when each risk peaks with interdependencies
Governance includes steering committee, decision authority, and escalation paths
Estimates use P50/P80/P95 ranges, never single numbers

evals.name: large-budget-governance
evals.prompt: Build an investment case for a $8M digital transformation over 24 months with multi-region deployment.
evals.expected_output: Roadmap triggers large-budget conditional logic: adds governance layer (PMO, steering committee, investment review board), quarterly re-calibration, and multi-region rollout wave planning.
expectations: Detects budget >$5M and adds governance layer with PMO and investment review board
Quarterly re-calibration gates present due to >12 month transformation
Multi-region deployment triggers rollout wave planning (low-risk to high-risk sequence)
Phased funding model with kill points at each phase gate
Break-even and ROI calculated with stated assumptions
Sensitivity analysis covers optimistic/likely/pessimistic/severe scenarios

evals.name: poc-invalidation-scenario
evals.prompt: Create a roadmap for migrating to event-driven architecture. Our team has no Kafka experience and the PoC may fail.
evals.expected_output: Roadmap accounts for high PoC failure risk: pivot decision framework applied, team skill gap addressed, kill criteria explicit for Phase 1.
expectations: Pivot decision framework includes PASS, minor FAIL, major FAIL, and showstopper paths
Team skill gap (no Kafka experience) addressed in Phase 1 with training roadmap
Kill criteria defined for Phase 1 gate if PoC invalidates >2 major assumptions
Estimation pivot points include Kafka performance validation as MUST-DO
Contingency budget allocated for pivot scenarios
Phase 2 explicitly conditional on Phase 1 PoC success

evals.name: executive-variant
evals.prompt: Create a roadmap for our cloud migration. Duration: 12 months, budget: under2m. Use variante ejecutiva.
evals.expected_output: Abbreviated roadmap (~40%) covering only S1 (transformation vision), S2 (phases), and S3 (investment horizon).
expectations: Output contains only sections S1, S2, and S3 (not full 7 sections)
Transformation vision includes success metrics and north star metric
Phases defined with gates even in abbreviated form
Investment horizon includes TCO and phased funding
Does not include full team roadmap, pivot points, risk management, or governance

evals.name: team-resistance-edge-case
evals.prompt: Plan the transformation for a legacy Java monolith. The development team is resistant to change and we have no PMO.
evals.expected_output: Roadmap triggers team resistance and no-PMO edge cases: extends Phase 1 for change management, embeds PM capacity, requires department head alignment.
expectations: Team resistance detected: Phase 1 extended by 1 month for change management
Department head alignment required before Phase 2 proceeds
No-PMO edge case: PM capacity embedded in Phase 1 and budgeted as permanent overhead
Change management track added as parallel workstream
Risk register includes team attrition and adoption failure scenarios
Roadmap is immediately actionable: steering committee can approve Phase 1 funding
